why_this_milestone_exists.purpose: Record the first completed five-game seeded RQ1 rerun suite and its interpretation against canonical best-policy scores.
why_this_milestone_exists.trigger: The rq1_seeded_20260428_s20260480 unified-suite run completed for Pong, Freeway, Asterix, Breakout, and Skiing.

results_vs_canonical.interpretation: Stable strong rerun, effectively matching the canonical best policy.
Successful but below the canonical best, showing some seed-to-seed variability.
Completed but did not rediscover the strong canonical Asterix behavior in this seed. This is important negative stability evidence.
Rerun exceeded the current canonical Breakout score and should be considered for canonical promotion only after standardized reevaluation.
Completed but below the canonical Skiing score. Because Skiing returns are negative, the more negative rerun score is worse.

RQ1.reason: One full seeded rerun suite now exists. It supports stability for Pong, gives a new strong Breakout candidate, and exposes weaker reruns for Freeway, Asterix, and Skiing. Additional seeds are still needed before estimating variance confidently.

RQ2.reason: No shortcut or robustness probes were run.

RQ3.reason: No task-variation adaptation experiment was run.

RQ4.reason: No new optimizer-ablation experiment was run.

interpretation.main_takeaway: The first RQ1 seeded rerun is scientifically useful because it is mixed rather than uniformly positive: LeGPS can still produce strong policies, but Asterix especially appears unstable under this seed/protocol.
positive_evidence: All five games completed under the same five-game unified-suite command.
Breakout exceeded the current canonical score and may be a candidate improvement after standardized reevaluation.
risk_evidence: Asterix did not reproduce its canonical strength.
Freeway and Skiing completed below canonical scores.
One rerun is not enough to estimate variance; it is only the first stability sample.

next_steps.step: Run at least two additional seeded RQ1 suites with new base seeds.
next_steps.why: Three or more seeded suites would make the variance and failure-rate discussion much more defensible.

next_steps.step: Standardize reevaluate the new Breakout rerun policy before promoting it to canonical best.
next_steps.why: The rerun score is higher than the current canonical score, but canonical promotion requires a separate saved-policy evaluation under the canonical protocol.

next_steps.step: Inspect Asterix policy/log differences to classify the stability failure.
next_steps.why: The Asterix drop is the largest result and matters directly for the method-stability claim.
